codeReview.summary: The implementation covers all 8 described fixes (D2-1 through D2-11) with good Python parity. 21 tests cover all VAL-M4-IB assertions. A few non-blocking deviations from Python were noted: combineImagesIntoPDF skips PDF ($565) resources instead of merging them via pypdf; the Go PDF writer is a custom minimal implementation rather than using a pypdf-equivalent library; and the old getOrderedImages (V1) method was left in place rather than being updated. These are acceptable given the project's constraints (Go has no direct pypdf equivalent and PDF resource merging requires significant external library support).

issues.description: combineImagesIntoPDF skips $565 PDF resources with a warning log instead of merging them. Python's combine_images_into_pdf (yj_to_image_book.py:228-236) handles PDF resources by merging same-location PDFs and using pypdf.PdfWriter.append(). This is a known limitation since Go has no direct pypdf equivalent. Acceptable for now but may need a follow-up when PDF merging support is added.

getOrderedImagesV2 duplicates the full landscape-splitting logic from getOrderedImages (V1). The V1 method still exists but returns only images (not pids/contentPosInfo). Ideally V1 should be deprecated or made to call V2 internally, but this is not blocking since V2 is the parity-correct version.

getResourceImage tilesRawMedia is built as a flat slice appended row-by-row, which matches Python's sequential tile_raw_media list construction. However, when a row in yjTilesRaw fails asSlice(), the code uses 'continue' which skips that row entirely in yjTiles but still leaves the loop. Python would raise an error for malformed tile data. Minor robustness difference, acceptable.

buildPDF has a two-pass approach that first creates page objects with IDs, then resets objID and rebuilds. The first pass creates objects that are discarded (objects[:0] reset at line 595). This is functional but the dead first-pass code from lines 566-585 is confusing. Not a bug but reduces readability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Go's combineImagesIntoPDF uses a custom minimal PDF writer (buildPDF/serializePDF) rather than a third-party PDF library. Python uses pypdf.PdfWriter for PDF merging, outline, and metadata. This is an architectural choice that should be documented in architecture.md since it affects future PDF-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/kfx/yj_to_image_book.go:496-710 — buildPDF creates raw PDF bytes manually. Python yj_to_image_book.py:215-294 uses pypdf.PdfWriter throughout.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature introduced getOrderedImagesV2 alongside the existing getOrderedImages, following a versioned API pattern. AGENTS.md doesn't document when to use V1 vs V2 methods or whether old methods should be updated to call new ones.

summary: PASS with non-blocking notes. All 8 Python parity fixes are implemented correctly with 21 passing tests. Key achievements: (1) getOrderedImagesV2 returns 3 values matching Python's (images, pids, contentPosInfo); (2) CBZ now converts PDF/JXR instead of skipping; (3) tile reassembly via combineImageTiles works correctly; (4) tocPageForPID implements the correct binary search; (5) cropImage scaling verified; (6) minimal PDF writer produces valid PDFs with metadata and outline. Non-blocking issues: PDF resource merging in combineImagesIntoPDF is skipped (no pypdf equivalent in Go), V1/V2 method duplication, and some dead code in buildPDF's two-pass approach.
